title: Streamline dump control options
body:
This patch reworks dumping control in line whit what we discussed in #3527. I have moved VL_DEBUG_FUNC (with a modified name and extended functionality) to the top of .cpp files (there is no need for one in each class), and added it to visitors as needed in .h files (as .h files might be included in .cpp files which themselves define these), and we now have `dump*()` functions generated by this macro.

- `dumpTree()` is the maximum of `--dumpi-tree` or `--dumpi-<srcfile>` 
- `dumpGraph()` is the maximum of `--dumpi-graph` or `--dumpi-<srcfile>` (used to be part of `--dump-treei` before)
- `dump()` is only affected by `--dumpi-<srcfile>`, so is appropriate for pass specific text files and similar

`dump` and `dumpTree` clash with a few existing names which fixed on a case by case basis. Could consider renaming this to something more specific (`doDump()` ?). There is also `AstNode::dumpTree`, but that is not ambiguous outside of an AstNode class, so is not a big issue.

I'm also contemplating tweaking these so instead of `if (dump() >= 2)` one would write `if (dump(2))` to avoid messes caused by liberal use of `>=` vs `>`.

As this is a developer option, I have not kept `--dump-treei` (which is now `--dumpi-tree --dumpi-graph`)

TODO:
- [x] Audit and fix other places that should use `if (dump())` instead of `if (debug())`
- [ ] Consider renaming `dump()` to `doDump()` (also `debug()` to `doDebug()` ?)
- [ ] Consider changing `if (dump() >= 2)` to `if (dump(2))`

@wsnyder if you are happy with this I will finish off and merge. The interesting files are `V3Option*`, `V3Error*`. `docs/*`, `bin/*`

It is now easy to add further `--dumpi-foo` options for new things.

---

- Rename `--dump-treei` option to `--dumpi-tree`, which itself is now a special case of `--dumpi-<tag>` where tag can be a magic word, or a filename
- Control dumping via static `dump*()` functions, analogous to `debug()`
- Make dumping independent of the value of `debug()` (so dumping always works even without the debug flag)
- Add separate `--dumpi-graph` for dumping V3Graphs, which is again a special case of `--dumpi-<tag>`
- Alias `--dump-<tag>` to `--dumpi-<tag> 3` as before
